topic.what: Extracting decision tree models from TEE-protected inference using microarchitectural side channels
topic.how: Exploiting branch prediction structures and performance counters to infer control-flow decisions during enclave inference
topic.why_matters: Demonstrates TEE-protected ML inference remains vulnerable; undermines confidential cloud ML services and model IP protection

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of queries required to fully extract decision tree
characteristics.vs_baseline: superior to prior black-box extraction attacks
characteristics.evidence: paper claims lowest query complexity for full tree reconstruction
characteristics.context: algorithm maximizes information per query via CFI coupling

characteristics.value: full reconstruction including duplicate feature usage
characteristics.vs_baseline: exceeds prior work that recovers only thresholds
characteristics.evidence: recovers features thresholds duplicate usage and internal node ordering
characteristics.context: shadow model matches target DT completely

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of tree nodes correctly extracted
characteristics.vs_baseline: outperforms prior attacks in realistic scenarios
characteristics.evidence: demonstrated on three libraries and two TEEs
characteristics.context: works without rich API output or fault injection assumptions

characteristics.vs_baseline: enables more efficient extraction than black-box only
characteristics.evidence: PHR and performance counters leak branching decisions
characteristics.context: 194 doublet PHR capacity on Intel; performance counters on AMD SEV

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of affected DT inference libraries
Number of TEE platforms successfully attacked
characteristics.evidence: Intel SGX and AMD SEV both vulnerable
characteristics.context: SGX uses PHR BHR; SEV uses SEV-Step performance counters
